name: Reasoning Depth Assessment - Logical Fallacies
description: Analyze 10 logical arguments and identify fallacies, validity, and reasoning quality. Each argument requires parsing structure, applying logical rules, detecting fallacies, and explaining reasoning. This is a psychometric benchmark using IRT principles - each reasoning step is individually scored for depth, self-correction, and verification behaviors.

preconditions: Each argument has 3-5 distinct reasoning steps
Arguments vary in difficulty (b-values from -1.5 to +1.5)
Scoring requires both final answer AND reasoning process quality

success_criteria: All 10 arguments correctly classified as valid/invalid
Fallacies correctly identified (undistributed middle, affirming consequent, etc.)
Reasoning depth score >= 2.0 on 0-3 scale (shows thorough analysis)
Self-correction score >= 1.5 (detects and corrects initial errors)
Verification score >= 1.5 (uses multiple approaches to verify conclusions)
Complete reasoning chains provided for each argument
IRT ability estimate (θ) can be calculated from step-level performance
